_comment_C_overview: PLAN C - {4,3,5} hyperbolic honeycomb corridor, 25 cube cells generated by BFS. Each cube is a regular H^3 cube with H^3 dihedral angle 2*pi/5 = 72deg (5 cubes meet at each edge -- impossible in euclidean R^3 where only 4 meet). The neighbour cells are produced by SO(3,1) reflections of the base cube through its 6 face-planes; each cube is hyperbolic-congruent to every other cube even though Klein coordinates make distant cells look exponentially smaller.

objects._comment: Chrome marble pulled toward camera and slid down. Sits in the lower foreground so it doesn't occlude the +x corridor (which carries the orange layer-1 lantern of the next cube down the spine). The reflection still mirrors the entire surrounding {4,3,5} lattice through hyperbolic geodesics, but now it's a foreground object rather than the whole frame.

Second chrome marble, upper-right foreground. Compare its reflection to the lower-left marble: the same 6 lanterns appear at NON-SYMMETRIC angles in the two reflections because the chord-reflection geometry depends on the marble's Klein position, not just its euclidean position.
